stimulus: Researchers believe that timbers recovered from a Florida riverbed are from a French merchant vessel that sank in 1565. Oral histories passed down among the area's Timucua descendants support this belief. ______blank Timucua accounts describe how barrels of wine, an item the ship had been carrying, drifted onto nearby shores in the months following the wreck.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "For example" logically signals that the Timucua wine-barrel accounts mentioned in this sentence are examples consistent with the previous claim that oral histories support the shipwreck theory.

Choice B is incorrect because "for this reason" illogically signals that the wine-barrel accounts were caused by the previous claim. Choice C is incorrect because "however" illogically signals contrast. Choice D is incorrect because "likewise" illogically signals that the wine-barrel accounts are similar to but separate from the supporting oral histories, when in fact they exemplify those histories.
